author: doll-agent
content:
Done. weighted_sample_with_replacement used a build-CDF + binary-search sampler (O(n) setup, O(log n) per sample). Replaced with Vose's alias method: O(n) setup, O(1) per sample (one uniform integer + one uniform float comparison).

Subtle correctness bug avoided: 'while let (Some(s), Some(l)) = (small.pop(), large.pop())' pops both stacks unconditionally and silently drops one element when the other is empty. Used explicit !small.is_empty() && !large.is_empty() guard. The existing choice_with_weights test caught this on the first run.

Tests added:
- weighted_with_replacement_alias_distribution_recovers_probs: 100k samples on uneven 5-bin distribution, empirical frequencies within 1.5% of target.
- weighted_with_replacement_unnormalized_probs: arbitrary-sum probs reproduce implied distribution.

ferray-random: 182 -> 184 tests, all passing, clippy clean. Commit 6d17adc7.
